name: Override Chart Values for Environments
description: Customize Helm chart behavior using values files and --set flags to manage configurations across development, staging, and production environments.

steps.title: View default chart values
steps.description: Inspect all configurable values and their defaults for a chart.
steps.tip: Redirect to a file so you can use it as a starting point for your custom values.

steps.title: Create an environment-specific values file
steps.description: Write a values file tailored for production settings.
steps.command:
cat > values-production.yaml << 'EOF'
replicaCount: 3
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 250m
    memory: 256Mi
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 512Mi
autoscaling:
  enabled: true
  minReplicas: 3
  maxReplicas: 10
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 75
EOF

steps.title: Install using a custom values file
steps.description: Deploy the chart with your environment-specific overrides.
steps.command: helm install my-nginx bitnami/nginx -f values-production.yaml --namespace production --create-namespace

steps.title: Layer multiple values files
steps.description: Combine a base config with environment-specific overrides. Later files take precedence.
steps.command: helm install my-nginx bitnami/nginx -f values-base.yaml -f values-production.yaml -f values-secrets.yaml
steps.tip: Files are merged left to right. The rightmost file wins for conflicting keys.

steps.title: Override individual values from the command line
steps.description: Set specific values without modifying files, useful for CI/CD.
steps.command: helm upgrade my-nginx bitnami/nginx -f values-production.yaml --set image.tag=1.25.3 --set service.type=LoadBalancer
steps.warning: Command-line --set values override everything, including values files. Use sparingly to keep configurations reproducible.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Helm Values Override Workflow
set -euo pipefail

CHART=${1:?"Usage: $0 <chart> <release> <environment>"}
RELEASE=${2:?"Missing release name"}
ENV=${3:?"Missing environment (dev|staging|production)"}

VALUES_FILE="values-$ENV.yaml"

if [ ! -f "$VALUES_FILE" ]; then
  echo "Error: $VALUES_FILE not found"
  echo "Creating template..."
  helm show values "$CHART" > "$VALUES_FILE"
  echo "Edit $VALUES_FILE and re-run this script"
  exit 1
fi

echo "=== Deploying $RELEASE to $ENV ==="
echo "Chart: $CHART"
echo "Values: $VALUES_FILE"

echo ""
echo "=== Rendered Values Preview ==="
helm template "$RELEASE" "$CHART" -f "$VALUES_FILE" --show-only templates/deployment.yaml 2>/dev/null | head -30

echo ""
echo "=== Installing/Upgrading ==="
helm upgrade --install "$RELEASE" "$CHART" \
  -f "$VALUES_FILE" \
  --namespace "$ENV" \
  --create-namespace \
  --atomic \
  --timeout 5m

echo ""
echo "=== Deployed Values ==="
helm get values "$RELEASE" -n "$ENV"

faq.question: What is the precedence order for values?
faq.answer: From lowest to highest priority: chart defaults (values.yaml) < parent chart values < -f files (left to right) < --set and --set-string flags. The last source wins.

faq.question: How do I set nested values with --set?
faq.answer: Use dot notation for nested keys: '--set server.resources.limits.cpu=500m'. For arrays use index notation: '--set servers[0].port=8080'. Escape dots in keys with a backslash.

faq.question: Can I validate my values file before deploying?
faq.answer: Yes. Use 'helm template <release> <chart> -f values.yaml' to render locally, or 'helm install --dry-run --debug' to validate against the cluster API without deploying.
